boundary_analysis.interpretation: The shared correlated-kernel timescale saturates near its registered 360-minute upper bound. Concentration by polynomial degree is an association, not proof that the polynomial and kernel compete.

mask_influence.reason: The frozen held-sector artifact contains joint vector log densities, not pointwise contributions. The two masks also retain different Phase-5B branch universes.

phase6r_residual_analysis.reconstruction: Deterministic residual evaluation at each frozen Phase-6R MAP endpoint; no optimizer, Hessian, Laplace draw, or model selection was run.

periodogram.reason: Branch frequency grids depend on each branch time baseline; peak summaries are descriptive and are not detection statistics.

versions.reason: All V1 optimizer attempts were unchanged from their initial values.
V2 stopped at 22/24 stationarity and did not run residual diagnostics.

questions.finding: OU, Matern-3/2, and SHO boundary folds are enumerated by branch and held sector in the boundary map.
Every boundary flag is the upper shared log-timescale bound; no amplitude or jitter boundary flag occurs.
Sector-level mask interactions are identified. Individual cadence predictive effects cannot be recovered without refitting or saved pointwise densities.
All 60 raw-only cadences are linked to sector, time, quality, background, pointing, and registered transit-window eligibility.
The Phase-6R beta excess is decomposed into all 24 branches, six sectors, and six registered timescales.
V1 ACF/beta/periodogram artifacts are quarantined numerical no-op products; V2 artifacts are empty. Valid Stage-3 summaries are derived separately from frozen Phase-6R endpoints.
Boundary saturation is associated with baseline degree, but current artifacts do not prove polynomial-kernel competition.
No complex kernel reached a joint transit/noise fit, so screening-to-joint instability cannot be measured from existing artifacts.
Frozen Phase-6R residual-telemetry correlations are reported without causal attribution. Cross-reduction residual comparison is unavailable because the joint model used only PDCSAP.
The existing screening artifacts cannot establish that a correlated kernel consumes ingress or egress because no complex-kernel joint transit fit exists.

supported_explanations.statement: Complex-kernel screening failures include shared timescale saturation at the registered upper bound.
Mask interaction is concentrated in identifiable held-sector folds, while its sign is negative for the aggregate of every complex kernel.
Phase-6R residual correlation is sector- and timescale-dependent, rather than a uniform excess scatter across sectors.

unsupported_explanations.statement: A particular one of the 60 cadences caused a predictive gain.
unsupported_explanations.reason: No pointwise predictive contributions or influence functions were saved.

unsupported_explanations.statement: The event polynomial definitively competed with the kernel.
unsupported_explanations.reason: The boundary-degree pattern is observational and no controlled refit exists.

unsupported_explanations.statement: A complex kernel would remain stable in the joint transit fit.
unsupported_explanations.reason: No complex kernel passed the frozen screening gate or reached that fit.

unsupported_explanations.statement: The red-noise source is a specific reduction or telemetry variable.
unsupported_explanations.reason: Correlations are descriptive and only the PDCSAP joint residual exists.

unsupported_explanations.statement: A complex kernel consumed transit ingress or egress.
unsupported_explanations.reason: No complex-kernel joint transit residual artifact exists.
